summary: Architecture review found the diff scope is confined to the task's allowed paths and no forbidden sim-core, save-format, content, Electron, ADR, gate, manifest, checksum, or validation-report paths were modified. Protocol/client-core additions stay on DTO/read-model projections and do not import WorldState into React/Redux. MapRenderer has render-plan and replace-read-model rebuild tests. However, the browser-visible map surface is implemented as React/SVG per-entity rendering in packages/ui/src/index.tsx, while apps/web only calls buildMapRenderPlan for an anchor count. That does not satisfy the task acceptance criterion requiring the web client to render through MapRenderer deltas, and it conflicts with ADR-008's React/Pixi boundary that React must not create one component per map entity.

tests_run.result: On feat/m2-client-map. Modified files are within allowed implementation/orchestration paths; untracked writer handoff, route messages, and tests/map-renderer-m2-map.test.ts are also within allowed project/messages or tests paths.
1 test file passed; 2 tests passed, covering seasonal/economy/routes render plans and replace-read-model cache rebuild.
Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.

decisions: REQUEST_CHANGES: Pixi mounting cannot be deferred as a follow-up for this task. M2-CLIENT-MAP-001 acceptance criterion 1 requires web rendering through read models and MapRenderer deltas, and ADR-008 states PixiJS imperatively owns maps, roads, regions, armies, and high-frequency labels while React must not create a component per map entity.

The protocol result DTO additions are acceptable in scope for this review because they mirror existing sim-core query output shapes and do not add new query kinds, save schema, sim-core rules, Electron IPC, RNG/time, or production dependency expansion.
The protocol-shaped client fixture remains a known non-blocking M2 risk until live worker query integration is scheduled; it is not the reason for this request-changes result.
risks: Current UI duplicates map presentation decisions already present in MapRenderer: districtFillColor, seasonalFillColor, routeStrokeColor, route width, label text, and per-entity SVG construction. This risks renderer/UI drift and bypasses MapRenderer ownership of render-plan semantics.
Current Playwright coverage proves the SVG/DOM surface works, not that the browser-visible map is rebuilt by MapRenderer/Pixi from a read model.
requested_action: Revise the implementation before acceptance: 1. Replace the React/SVG per-district/per-route/per-settlement visible map in packages/ui/src/index.tsx with a MapRenderer-owned rendered surface in the web shell. React may own only UI intent state such as mode, zoom, selected entity, filters, sorting, and panel/list state. 2. The visible map must receive read-model replacement through MapRenderer rebuild/applyDelta semantics and a viewport containing mode, zoom, and selected entity. Under the current ADR, this should be Pixi-backed; do not defer Pixi mounting unless lead first creates/approves an explicit architecture deviation. 3. Remove duplicated map presentation formulas from UI and keep color, route width, label, and selected visual instruction generation in packages/map-renderer. 4. Keep dependency direction narrow: sim-core remains untouched; map-renderer must not import React/UI; no Electron IPC, save/schema, production dependency, RNG/time, or domain rule changes. 5. Update focused tests and Playwright coverage so they exercise the browser-visible renderer-owned surface plus zoom, district/settlement selection, seasonal/economy/routes modes, route previews, selected details, and rebuild from read model.
